page.primary_keyword: New Year's Day yacht charter Marbella
page.title: New Year's Day Yacht Charter in Marbella: Start the Year at Sea

data.meta_description: Start 2025 with a New Year's Day yacht charter in Marbella. Cruise the Costa del Sol from €749/2h. Book your boat from Puerto Banús today.
data.summary: Celebrate New Year's Day with a luxury yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Our fleet offers skippered trips from €749/2h, including drinks and snacks, for a memorable start to the year at sea.
key_facts: Charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella.
Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39).
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and IVA.
Mangusta 80 (24m) available from €4,719 for 4 hours with free jet ski.
Maximum 12 guests on most yachts; smaller boats for 5–6 pax.
New Year's Day weather averages 16–18°C on the Costa del Sol.
Book via WhatsApp for custom durations and boat options.

faq.q: Can I book a New Year's Day yacht charter in Marbella on short notice?
faq.a: Yes, we recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, but last-minute availability is possible. Contact us via WhatsApp to check same-day options. Popular boats like the Azimut 39 and Mangusta 80 fill quickly for New Year's Day.

faq.q: What is included in the New Year's Day charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs—just show up at Puerto Banús.

faq.q: How long are New Year's Day charters?
faq.a: We offer flexible durations from 2 hours to full day (8 hours). The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. For other boats, pricing is on request.

faq.q: What is the weather like for a New Year's Day boat trip in Marbella?
faq.a: Marbella enjoys mild winters. New Year's Day typically sees temperatures of 16–18°C with clear skies. The sea is calm, making for a comfortable cruise. Bring a light jacket for the breeze.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on the yacht?
faq.a: Our standard package includes drinks and light snacks. You are welcome to bring additional food or a picnic, but glass bottles are discouraged for safety. Let us know your preferences when booking.

faq.q: Which yachts are best for a small group on New Year's Day?
faq.a: For up to 5 guests, the Dubhe (8m) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m) are ideal. For 6–8 guests, consider the Bandido or Speedboat. Larger groups of 10–12 fit well on the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80.

faq.q: Is there a minimum age for passengers on New Year's Day charters?
faq.a: No minimum age, but children must be supervised at all times. Life jackets are available for all sizes. The skipper will brief everyone on safety before departure.

faq.q: What if the weather is bad on New Year's Day?
faq.a: We monitor conditions closely. If the forecast is unsafe, we offer a full refund or reschedule to another day. Most New Year's Days in Marbella are sunny and calm.

data.body_html:
Forget the hangover and the leftovers—start the new year with a New Year's Day yacht charter in Marbella. While most people are nursing a coffee, you can be cruising the Costa del Sol aboard a luxury motor yacht, with the Mediterranean sun on your face and a glass of cava in hand. Departing from Puerto Banús, our fleet offers skippered trips from just €749 for two hours, including drinks and snacks. It's the most civilised way to welcome 2025.

Why Choose a New Year's Day Yacht Charter in Marbella?

Marbella in January is mild, calm, and uncrowded. The average temperature sits at 16–18°C, the sea is flat, and the marinas are quiet. A yacht charter in Marbella on New Year's Day lets you skip the tourist crowds and experience the coastline from the water. You'll see the Sierra Blanca mountains dusted with snow, the Golden Mile stretched out behind you, and the iconic yachts of Puerto Banús bobbing at their moorings. It's a peaceful, luxurious start to the year.

Our Fleet for New Year's Day

We have boats for every group size and budget. All depart from Puerto Banús and include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks. Here are the highlights:

BoatLengthMax PaxPrice from
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719/4h
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On request
Dubhe8m5On request

For a full list, visit our fleet page. The Mangusta 80 (white or grey) includes a free jet ski for the day—perfect for some New Year's fun on the water.

What's Included in Your New Year's Day Charter

Every booking comes with:

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the entire trip
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (olives, crisps, nuts)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21% tax)

No hidden extras. Just turn up at Puerto Banús and step aboard.

Best Routes for a New Year's Day Cruise

Your skipper will tailor the route to the weather, but popular options include:

  • West to Estepona: Cruise past the luxury villas of the Golden Mile, the fishing port of Cabopino, and the rugged coastline to Estepona's marina. About 12 NM each way—perfect for a 4-hour trip.
  • East to Cala del Faro: Head towards Marbella's eastern coves, including the secluded Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés. Anchor for a swim (yes, some brave souls do jump in January).
  • South to the Strait: For longer charters (6–8 hours), venture towards Sotogrande or even the Bay of Gibraltar. Spot dolphins and enjoy open-sea views.

Ask your skipper for recommendations—they know the best spots for the day.

How to Book Your New Year's Day Yacht Charter

Booking is simple. Choose your boat from our fleet and contact us via WhatsApp for availability. For the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, you can book directly at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours. For all other boats, we'll provide a custom quote. We recommend booking at least 48 hours ahead, especially for the popular Mangusta 80.

Tips for a Perfect New Year's Day on the Water

  • Dress in layers: 16°C feels cooler on the water. Bring a windproof jacket.
  • Sunscreen: The winter sun can still burn—SPF 30+ is wise.
  • Camera: The light is stunning in January, with low sun and clear skies.
  • Arrive early: Puerto Banús can be quiet, but parking is easy. Aim for 10:00 AM for a morning cruise.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can I book a New Year's Day yacht charter in Marbella on short notice?

Yes, we recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, but last-minute availability is possible. Contact us via WhatsApp to check same-day options. Popular boats like the Azimut 39 and Mangusta 80 fill quickly for New Year's Day.

What is included in the New Year's Day charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs—just show up at Puerto Banús.

How long are New Year's Day charters?

We offer flexible durations from 2 hours to full day (8 hours). The Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours. The Mangusta 80 requires a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719. For other boats, pricing is on request.

What is the weather like for a New Year's Day boat trip in Marbella?

Marbella enjoys mild winters. New Year's Day typically sees temperatures of 16–18°C with clear skies. The sea is calm, making for a comfortable cruise. Bring a light jacket for the breeze.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on the yacht?

Our standard package includes drinks and light snacks. You are welcome to bring additional food or a picnic, but glass bottles are discouraged for safety. Let us know your preferences when booking.

Which yachts are best for a small group on New Year's Day?

For up to 5 guests, the Dubhe (8m) or Mariah SX21 (6.4m) are ideal. For 6–8 guests, consider the Bandido or Speedboat. Larger groups of 10–12 fit well on the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80.

Is there a minimum age for passengers on New Year's Day charters?

No minimum age, but children must be supervised at all times. Life jackets are available for all sizes. The skipper will brief everyone on safety before departure.

What if the weather is bad on New Year's Day?

We monitor conditions closely. If the forecast is unsafe, we offer a full refund or reschedule to another day. Most New Year's Days in Marbella are sunny and calm.

Start 2025 the right way. Book your New Year's Day yacht charter in Marbella today and make memories that last the whole year.
